alternative_translations: one who has entered the stream
definition: A person who has entered the stream to awakening and irreversibly shifted in view and trajectory.
notes: The project keeps stream-enterer because it is the clearest compact doctrinal label in English. The longer phrase one who has entered the stream can be used where a less technical sentence shape is needed.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose where the sentence benefits from an explicit relative clause
context_rules.rendering: one who has entered the stream

example_phrases.notes: Common inflected form in descriptions of stage qualities.
